title: La stimulation épidurale dans le traitement des douleurs chroniques
study_question: Can epidural stimulation treat chronic pain in failed disc syndrome patients?
population_assessed: 20 patients with failed disc syndrome after multiple laminectomies
outcome_measures: Pain relief, analgesic use, physical activity, complications
inclusion_criteria: Failed disc syndrome after multiple laminectomies; chronic pain requiring treatment
answer_to_study_question: Good results in 50%; better for unilateral pain; complications included migration and infection.
reasoning: Anthropic and Google strongly agreed on bibliographic fields, design, year, and sample size. I preferred Anthropic's issue detail and merged both clinical summaries; OpenRouter provided no usable evidence.
field_level_decisions_json: {"volume_issue_pages": "Used Anthropic's more specific issue notation over Google's volume-only entry.", "doi": "No DOI supplied by reliable models; kept as NAN.", "study_question": "Merged both models' equivalent failed disc syndrome formulations.", "outcome_measures": "Combined shared efficacy outcomes with Anthropic's reported complications.", "inclusion_criteria": "Used overlapping chronic failed-disc criteria, excluding less-certain detoxification detail.", "answer_to_study_question": "Merged consensus 50% benefit with unilateral-pain advantage and noted reported complications."}
